dialogue.utterance: Patch to Unit 3 for a suspected Stroke/CVA at --Address Redacted-- in the city of Richmond.
Hi, I'm Javier, a paramedic with Hazmat. What brought you to call for help today?
I've been dizzy and have back pain after two falls today. Also, my speech has been slurred on and off since yesterday.
Per the family, you had some confusion. Are you aware of where you are or what day it is?
You are Alert but not fully Oriented, with a GCS of 14. Your verbal responses show some confusion.
We're assessing your airway. No signs of obstruction or abnormal sounds.
You're breathing at 20 a minute and equal chest rise.
Your pulse is 71, strong and regular. Your blood pressure is 105/71.
A cervical collar has been applied to stabilize your neck after your falls.
Oxygen saturation is 98%, and we initiated 4-lead monitoring. A 12-lead was performed showing Atrial Fibrillation and Right Bundle Branch Block.
Initial blood sugar was 130, but later reads were not completed.
Your blood pressure dropped to 96/54 initially, then rose to 100/59, while your breathing remained steady at 20 per minute.
You showed tachycardia, with pulses climbing from 110 to 116.
Your EKG remains stable with atrial fibrillation, RBBB, and axis deviations. Sats stayed at 100%, then dropped to 97%.
Your skin is warm, pink, and dry. No trauma noted.
You have a history of stroke and are diabetic. Were you on any blood thinners?
I didn't take any of my meds lately. I'm not sure if I was on blood thinners.
No known allergies in your record. No evidence of rash or reaction.
The family said you had an episode last night with slurred speech and again after your second fall this morning, and you had loss of consciousness.
You were walking with family when you had another episode of slurred speech after a fall this morning, followed by another fall.
Your skin turgor is normal. No rashes or bite marks noted.
PERRL for pupils with no neck rigidity or masses. Your temporomandibular joint was not swollen or tender.
No nasal or ear drainage. Your mouth was clear and your voice showed slurring but no abnormal obstruction.
Lung sounds are clear on all fields.
Heart sounds consistent with EKG findings: Atrial Fibrillation, RBBB, and left axis deviation.
Abdomen is soft and non-tender. No masses or tenderness found.
No bladder or rectal bleeding. No urinary complaints.
No angulation or deformities noted. Extremities are intact with strong pulses.
We started an IV and gave 200 mL of Normal Saline per protocol for support.
You reported back pain from the falls as your worst discomfort.
The pain began after the first fall and described as aching. No alleviating factors mentioned.
No pallor noted, but you appear pale compared to baseline per family report.
Family reported unilateral weakness previously, but this was not observed during our exam.
Given the abnormal EKG and history consistent with CVA, we're transporting to VCU for stroke protocol and further evaluation.
You were placed on the stretcher and secured with a five-point harness to avoid re-injury.
ED, this 62-year-old male was transported from the scene with a suspected CVA. Key findings: slurred speech, a fall, and abnormal EKG.
Understood. The patient is on a stretcher and awaiting transfer to the ED bed.
He had a known stroke one month ago and diagnosed diabetes. Cervical collar on board. 12-lead obtained showing A Fib with RBBB.
Noted. Move your patient over and I'll take over care.
Initial vitals were 105/71, HR 71, RR 20. Pulse escalated to 116, and oxygen sat dropped to 97%.
Understood, we'll do CT and initiate stroke protocol given the questionable CVA.
His initial GCS was 14, Alert but confused. Glucose was 130 followed by non-obtainable readings.
Thanks for the heads-up. Blood thinners are still unknown due to the inability to confirm with patient.
Per protocol 3-15 medical: Altered mental status. EKG findings were reviewed, with no ST elevation noted.
We followed Medical Protocol #3-15 for Altered Mental Status based on your symptoms and vitals.
